label: Negation
description: Saying no, not, never, nobody: where non goes, the two-part negatives, and the places Italian negates differently from English.

label: The non system
description: Sentence negation with non and its two-part partners.

label: Where non goes
description: Non sits before the verb, and before any clitics: Non lo vedo, non mi piace. Nothing comes between non and the clitic-verb block.

attributes.common_miss: Lo non vedo (clitic before non) and io non... word-order calques.

label: Two-part negatives: non... mai, più, ancora
description: The non...X frames: non...mai (never), non...più (no longer), non...ancora (not yet), non...affatto / per niente (not at all). Simple-tense placement lives here; placement inside compound tenses belongs to the passato prossimo tree.

attributes.common_miss: Dropping non (bevo mai), or English-order mai bevo.

label: Double negation is the norm
description: Negative concord: nessuno, niente, nulla after the verb KEEP non (Non viene nessuno; Non ho visto niente); moved before the verb they DROP it (Nessuno viene). The forms and meanings of the negative indefinites themselves will live with the indefinites topic; this leaf owns the word-order rule.

attributes.canonical_example: Non ho visto nessuno. / Nessuno mi ha visto.
attributes.common_miss: Non nessuno è venuto (keeping non with a preverbal negative), or dropping non postverbally on the English single-negation instinct.

label: Neanche, nemmeno, neppure
description: Negative agreement and echo answers: neanche/nemmeno/neppure io, Neanch'io!, Neanche a me (the piacere echo). All three are interchangeable; elision to neanch' before io.

attributes.canonical_example: Non mi piace il pesce. Neanche a me!
attributes.common_miss: Anche io as the echo to a negative (needs neanche).

label: Non... che: only
description: The restrictive frame non...che = only (Non ho che dieci euro), against solo; recognising it in reading is the first skill, producing it the second.

attributes.common_miss: Reading non...che as a real negation.

label: Pleonastic non
description: The non that does not negate: finché non, a meno che non, and the comparative più di quanto non + subjunctive.

label: Finché and finché non
description: Under the until reading, non is optional and changes nothing (Studiavo finché (non) mi sono addormentato). Under the as-long-as reading, adding non REVERSES the meaning (Sono stato bene finché ho abitato a Roma vs finché non ho abitato a Roma). Classifying which non this is IS the skill.

attributes.canonical_example: Sono stato bene finché non ho abitato a Roma.
attributes.common_miss: Reading the pleonastic non as a negation, or missing the meaning flip under the as-long-as reading.

label: A meno che non, and friends
description: A meno che non + subjunctive (unless), and the comparative pleonastic (più difficile di quanto non pensassi). The non is structural, not semantic.

attributes.canonical_example: Vengo, a meno che non piova.
attributes.common_miss: Omitting the non, or double-translating it (unless it does NOT rain).

label: Mica
description: Colloquial reinforcement with three position-meaning pairings: after the verb reinforcing non (Non ho mica detto...), before the verb replacing non (Mica sono stupida!), and question-softening supposition (Mica hai visto mia madre?).

attributes.canonical_example: Non ho mica detto che devi farlo!
attributes.common_miss: Mica placed before the verb WITH non, or read as a noun.
